input.prompt:
I'm migrating my test project from MSTest v2 to MSTest v3. The project has a
.testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout, and a code coverage
data collector. After the MSTest v3 upgrade, the .testsettings file seems to be ignored.
How do I migrate this configuration as part of the MSTest v2 to v3 upgrade?

data.content:

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <MSTest>
    <!-- Migrated from .testsettings TestTimeout property -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- Assembly resolution with useLoadContext is the default in modern MSTest; no extra config needed -->
  </MSTest>
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Migrated from .testsettings CodeCoverage data collector -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
        uri="datacollector://microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
        assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.dll">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.reasoningText:
The user has:

1. A `.csproj` using MSTest 3.8.0 (already upgraded the package)
2. A `local.testsettings` file with:
   - Assembly resolution / test deployment config
   - A `TestTimeout` of 30000ms (30 seconds)
   - A Code Coverage data collector

In MSTest v3, `.testsettings` files are no longer supported. The configuration needs to be migrated to a `.runsettings` file and/or MSTest v3 attributes/properties.

Let me provide guidance on how to migrate each piece:

1. **Test deployment / assembly resolution**: In MSTest v3, `DeploymentItem` attribute still works, but the `<TestDirectory useLoadContext="true" />` is the default behavior in modern .NET testing. 

2. **TestTimeout (30000ms)**: I can set this via the `[Timeout(30000)]` attribute on individual tests, or configure it globally in a `.runsettings` file under `<MSTest><TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout></MSTest>`, plus there are separate timeout settings for assembly, class, and method initialization.

3. **Code Coverage data collector**: This configuration moves into the `.runsettings` file under `<DataCollectionRunSettings>`. I'm going to create a `.runsettings` file that consolidates all these settings and update the project accordingly.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <MSTest>
    <!-- Migrated from .testsettings TestTimeout property -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- Assembly resolution with useLoadContext is the default in modern MSTest; no extra config needed -->
  </MSTest>
  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <!-- Migrated from .testsettings CodeCoverage data collector -->
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
        uri="datacollector://microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
        assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.dll">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content: Now wire up the `.runsettings` in the project file:

Here's a summary of the migration:

**MSTest v3 dropped `.testsettings` support.** You need a `.runsettings` file instead. I created `local.runsettings` and wired it into the `.csproj`.

Here's how each setting was migrated:

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `<Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" />` | `<MSTest><TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout></MSTest>` |
| Code Coverage `DataCollector` | `<DataCollectionRunSettings><DataCollectors>` section (updated to v2.0 URI) |
| `<TestDirectory useLoadContext="true" />` | No longer needed — this is the default behavior in modern MSTest |

The old `local.testsettings` file can be safely deleted once you've verified the new configuration works. You can also set per-test timeouts with `[Timeout(ms)]` if needed.
